sources_exist.note: All seven canonical URLs resolved to the cited pages or PDF and were accessible for claim review.

figures_match_sources.note: Every score-affecting research chain has a material defect: the consolidator source does not establish PE backing, the buy-multiple evidence dates to 2003, the adoption and historical-analog sources do not measure firm AI adoption or a 50% technology transition, the owner source does not contain the quoted percentages or code-specific succession finding, the exit page omits the asserted private transaction, and the pricing page omits the stated direct/programmatic mix and take rate.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: pi_moat's physical-scarcity logic is plausible and all declared inputs are bounded, but ai_replaceable_share does not actually re-weight the contaminated 5418 proxy: it keeps the full 8.29% PR and 6.55% research wage shares despite saying these roles are largely absent in OOH operators, then automates both at 70%. The unlisted-role rationale computes a 0.2864 fraction but sets 0.22 as a supposed rounding, so the declared role judgment is internally inconsistent.

rubric_consistent.note: The deterministic dataset copy matches. The assigned ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag reflects only small stored-versus-recomputed presentation deltas on A, B, M, S, and V and is not independently score-affecting, but B violates the frozen definition because active_consolidators requires PE-backed platforms while the record explicitly counts three non-Big-Three operators with no identified PE backing. The stale and non-comparable multiple chains also fail the EBITDA-comparability requirements.

cross_checks_honest.note: Terminal-value and no-proxy answers are directionally candid, but the adoption rationale uses DOOH revenue and programmatic transaction penetration as proxies for AI adoption among firms, contrary to the required current_adoption_pct definition. Pricing-model deflation asserts 59% direct volume, 25% total OOH through programmatic, and a 15-20% platform take rate that the cited page does not provide.

source_audits.note: The 2025 transaction list documents many OOH buyers and repeated acquisitions by Adams, Lamar, Huntington, Mashburn, YESCO, and others. It neither states roughly 70 transactions nor establishes that the record's three counted regional consolidators are PE-backed; the record itself admits no explicitly PE-backed platforms were identified, which is incompatible with the rubric input.

The article supports historical 8x-12x EBITDA multiples for good sign inventories and about 10x forward EBITDA for Lamar, but it was published in November 2003 and explicitly discusses the preceding few years. It is far beyond the 12-month staleness threshold and does not support a current 2026 9x small-operator buy multiple.

OAAA reports $9.46B of 2025 OOH revenue and DOOH at 36.3% of OOH revenue. It does not report AI adoption among 541850 firms, a 70% programmatic share of North American DOOH, or 59% of marketers buying only through direct deals, and therefore cannot support current_adoption_pct=0.20.

The page supports December 2024 public trading comps of 16.1x estimated EBITDA for Lamar, 12.5x for OUTFRONT, and 11x for Clear Channel. It does not mention Blackstone/New Tradition at 18x and does not establish a 14x private $10M-plus EBITDA platform exit; public REIT and highly leveraged issuer comps require a documented normalization that is absent.

The PDF reports DOOH at 36% of 2025 OOH sales, programmatic DOOH at 30% of DOOH, and total OOH spend from 2009-2025. It does not show DOOH penetration near zero in 2009, a measured 15-year transition, or expected 50% penetration in the mid-2020s; it also conflicts with the record's separate 70% programmatic-DOOH assertion.

The page states that 2.9 million US businesses are owned by people 55 or older and gives generic exit-planning survey statistics. It does not contain the quoted 52.3%, 29.5%, or 22.8% age split, does not measure owners age 60-plus, and provides no OOH-specific evidence that a succession shortage is documented.

The article explains AI-enabled and programmatic OOH and states that private marketplaces represented 96% of programmatic OOH transactions in H1 2025. It does not state that 59% of marketers buy only through direct deals, that 41% of volume is programmatic, or that platforms take 15-20%, so the claimed billing mix and capture deduction are unsupported.

reasons: At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://billboardinsider.com/out-of-home-acquisition-list-for-2024-2/), identify PE sponsors and evidence of add-ons in the last 24 months for each counted platform, or set a properly researched fallback. Counting active strategic/regional buyers while admitting no PE backing conflicts with the frozen active_consolidators definition and affects B.

At inputs.buy_mult.url (https://www.signvalue.com/appraising-billboard-companies/), replace the November 2003 valuation article with recent closed small-operator transaction evidence normalized to EBITDA after market-rate owner compensation; the stale historical 8x-12x range cannot support a current 9x buy multiple.

At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://oaaa.org/news/out-of-home-advertising-revenue-reaches-record-9-46-billion/), provide evidence measuring the share of 541850 firms materially using AI in automatable functions. DOOH revenue penetration is not firm AI adoption and does not support value 0.20.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://oaaa.org/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/2025-OOH-Ad-Spend-Performance-Facts-and-Figures-FINAL.pdf), provide consistent annual DOOH penetration data establishing both the starting share and time to approximately 50%; total OOH spend and a 2025 endpoint alone cannot support the 15-year analog or t50_years=7.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://project-equity.org/news/employee-ownership-insider/business-owner-statistics-exit-planning/), provide a source for an age-60-plus owner share and code-specific evidence of a succession shortage before applying the OW bonus. The current source contains neither the quoted percentages nor an OOH finding.

At inputs.exit_mult.url (https://billboardinsider.com/public-out-of-home-company-valuations-at-december-2024/), document the asserted Blackstone transaction and normalize current public REIT/issuer comps to a private $10M-plus EBITDA platform; the cited page does not itself support exit_mult=14.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://www.emarketer.com/content/faq-on-ai-ooh-advertising--how-artificial-intelligence-reshaping-channel-2026), source the direct-versus-programmatic share and platform take rate used in pi_dist and the pricing-deflation cross-check; the current page provides neither.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, M, S, V
